decks.notes: The main concept of this deck is to utilize aggressive creatures and cheap, efficient spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent and deal damage. By combining cards that provide card draw, bounce effects, and direct damage with efficient creatures like Eddymurk Crab and Slickshot Show-Off, the deck aims to quickly build up a board presence and apply pressure. The inclusion of versatile spells like Burst Lightning and Prismari Charm provides additional ways to control the board and push through damage, while also offering flexibility in dealing with various threats.

The main focus of this deck is to control the opponent's resources through a combination of counterspells, removal, and bounce effects, while slowly building up card advantage and controlling the board with creatures like Eddymurk Crab and Hearth Elemental. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and outvalue them in the long run, with key cards like Opt and Prismari Charm providing card selection and versatility. The deck also includes a small burn package with Burst Lightning and Flow State to provide additional reach and removal options.

The main concept of this Izzet Control deck is to stall the opponent's game plan using various counter spells, removal spells, and bounce effects, while generating card advantage through card draw effects like Tablet of Discovery and Consult the Star Charts. The deck aims to control the board state and tempo of the game by utilizing versatile spells like Prismari Charm and Burst Lightning. It also includes powerful finishers like Colorstorm Stallion and Sanar, Unfinished Genius to close out the game once the opponent's resources have been depleted.

The main idea of this deck is to overwhelm your opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy using a mix of efficient creatures and combat tricks. The deck focuses on playing early threats like Llanowar Elves and Badgermole Cub, while also utilizing cards like Brightglass Gearhulk and Leatherhead, Swamp Stalker to maintain board presence. The inclusion of Practiced Offense and Bushwhack provides additional reach and surprise burst damage to quickly close out games. With a solid mana base and a mix of creatures and combat tricks, this deck aims to outpace and outmaneuver the opponent for a swift victory.

The main focus of this Izzet Control deck is to control the game through a combination of countering enemy spells, removing threats with burn spells, and drawing cards to maintain card advantage. The deck utilizes a mix of instants, sorceries, and creatures to disrupt the opponent's game plan while slowly building up their own resources. The key is to carefully manage resources and make optimal plays to outlast opponents and secure victory through gradual advantage accumulation.

This deck focuses on controlling the opponent's threats and disrupting their game plan with a mix of removal spells, counter magic, and card draw. The deck uses cards like Abrade and Burst Lightning to deal with creatures, while cards like Traumatic Critique and Winternight Stories disrupt the opponent's hand size and tempo. The deck also aims to stabilize the board with cards like Hearth Elemental and Riverpyre Verge. Overall, the deck seeks to outlast the opponent and win through incremental advantage.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through a combination of removal spells, card draw, and counterspells, while using powerful finishers like Jeskai Revelation. The deck aims to accumulate knowledge and resources through cards like Accumulate Wisdom and Tablet of Discovery, then strategically use them to outplay the opponent. It also utilizes the Izzet color combination to efficiently control the board with cards like Firebending Lesson and Combustion Technique. The deck's strategy is to outlast the opponent and overwhelm them with powerful spells and abilities.

This Selesnya Aggro deck focuses on utilizing efficient creatures and combat tricks to overwhelm the opponent quickly. With a low mana curve and a mix of aggressive creatures like Badgermole Cub and Surrak, Elusive Hunter, along with powerful support cards like Brightglass Gearhulk and Gene Pollinator, this deck aims to apply early pressure and close out the game before the opponent can stabilize. By combining creature aggression with combat tricks like Practiced Offense and Seam Rip, this deck aims to maintain board control and push damage through to secure a swift victory.

The key focus of this deck is to quickly generate aggression through a combination of creatures with flying and spells that can target opponent's creatures or deal direct damage, while also maintaining control through bouncing effects and card draw. The deck aims to apply pressure early in the game with small evasive creatures like Colorstorm Stallion and Slickshot Show-Off, while using spells like Burst Lightning and Boomerang Basics to control the board. The deck also benefits from card draw and tempo plays to maintain its aggressive stance throughout the game.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by disrupting the opponent's strategy with counterspells and removal spells while also generating card advantage through cards like Opt and Sleight of Hand. The deck aims to maintain board presence with creatures like Eddymurk Crab and Hearth Elemental, while also being able to deal direct damage with spells like Burst Lightning and Prismari Charm. The deck also has options for tempo plays with cards like Get Out and Spell Pierce, making it versatile in different matchups.

The main concept of this Izzet Control deck is to establish board control through a combination of counter spells and removal, while also generating card advantage through card draw and manipulation effects. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and gradually build towards a win condition by out-valuing them in the mid to late game. The inclusion of versatile cards like Prismari Charm and Opt provide flexibility in dealing with various threats, while cards like Sunderflock and Traumatic Critique help to maintain control over the game state.

The main concept behind this Izzet Control deck is to carefully manage the board with a mix of counterspells, removal, and card draw. The deck aims to control the pace of the game by using cards like Opt and Sleight of Hand to filter through the deck, while utilizing spells like Burst Lightning and Prismari Charm to remove threats. Eddymurk Crab and Sunderflock provide additional ways to disrupt the opponent's strategy, while cards like Impractical Joke and Winternight Stories offer flexibility in dealing with various threats. Overall, the deck focuses on outsmarting and outmaneuvering opponents to secure victory.

This deck focuses on using card draw, counterspells, and removal to control the board and out value the opponent. It aims to stall the game out until it can overwhelm the opponent with powerful spells and creatures, while disrupting their game plan with counterspells and removal. The deck is designed to have a mix of reactive and proactive elements, allowing it to adapt to different matchups and strategies. By carefully managing resources and timing spells effectively, the deck can outmaneuver and outlast its opponents.

This Jeskai Control deck focuses on utilizing a variety of spells to control the game, including counterspells, removal, card draw, and burn spells. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while generating card advantage and maintaining board control. The deck also includes some versatile spells like Prismari Charm and Into the Flood Maw, which can provide additional options based on the situation. The mana base is designed to support the deck's color requirements while also providing flexibility in playing spells with different mana costs.

The deck focuses on playing aggressive creatures and overwhelming the opponent with fast attacks. It makes use of a mix of strong creatures like Brightglass Gearhulk and Surrak, Elusive Hunter, supported by cards like Practiced Offense and Keen-Eyed Curator to maintain pressure. The inclusion of low-cost creatures like Llanowar Elves and Badgermole Cub allows for early board presence and ramping into bigger threats. The mana base is designed to ensure consistency in playing creatures and spells efficiently. Overall, the deck aims to quickly build a strong board presence and push for a fast victory.

This deck focuses on using a combination of cards to bring powerful creatures back from the graveyard, allowing for explosive plays and overwhelming the opponent. It includes removal spells, hand disruption, and ways to refill your hand while setting up your reanimation strategy. The key strategy involves carefully managing resources and timing your plays to set up big swings in momentum. By efficiently using your graveyard and manipulating your hand size, you can quickly turn the tide of the game in your favor and secure victory.
